Title: WHIRLY1 regulates aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis in early seedling development of Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WHIRLY family; plant-specific single-stranded DNA/RNA-binding protein with Whirly domain (Pfam PF08536); transcription regulator
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast-targeted; also described as acting in plant genetic compartments
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function examined in 5-day-old seedlings; effect also observed in seeds but not mature rosette leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sulfur-containing defense metabolites; also nutrient reservoir/remobilization source during seed-to-seedling transition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of WHIRLY1 decreases aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic gene expression and lowers aliphatic glucosinolate contents in seedlings and seeds, without altering myrosinase activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout (why1-4, why1-5); RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS glucosinolate profiling; myrosinase activity assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early seedling development under continuous light on half-strength MS medium with 1% sucrose; 22±2°C; 3 days stratification; 5 DAG analyzed
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 construct used p6i-d35S-TE9 with promoter context not further detailed for metabolic testing
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1101217; metabolomics by LC-MS/MS glucosinolate profiling
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter W-box and IR2 motifs in target genes suggested as potential WHIRLY1-binding-associated regulatory sites; possible promoter epigenetic effects discussed but not experimentally validated
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 loss-of-function of AtWHIRLY1 specifically suppressed multiple genes in methionine-derived aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis in 5-day-old seedlings. 2. Knockout plants had reduced total and especially short-chain aliphatic glucosinolate levels in seedlings, and altered aliphatic glucosinolate composition in seeds. 3. Myrosinase activity was unchanged, supporting a primary role of WHIRLY1 in biosynthesis rather than glucosinolate degradation. 4. The regulatory effect was seedling-stage specific and not evident in mature rosettes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate aliphatic glucosinolate content/composition during early development and seed traits in Brassicales; should be deployed with caution because WHIRLY1 also has broader developmental/organelle functions and stage specificity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious seedling growth or photosynthetic penalty under standard conditions in Arabidopsis; possible broader effects on stress/developmental gene networks
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats WHIRLY1 as the central experimentally validated gene. Other genes in the pathway were mainly transcript/metabolic readouts rather than individually functionally tested in this study. why1-1 T-DNA mutant did not reproduce the same transcriptional phenotype as CRISPR null alleles.
